area_description: Reinforcement Learning for LLMs encompasses techniques for post-training alignment of large language models using human feedback, preference optimization, reward modeling, and iterative self-improvement, spanning the full RLHF pipeline, direct preference methods, RL algorithm design, alignment strategies, and self-play approaches.

categories.name: RLHF Pipeline
categories.description: The classical Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback pipeline, including collecting human preference data, training reward models from comparisons, and optimizing language model policies using RL algorithms such as PPO against learned reward signals.

sub_topics.name: Reward Modeling
sub_topics.description: Training reward models from human preference comparisons to serve as proxy objectives for RL-based policy optimization.

sub_topics.name: PPO-based Policy Training
sub_topics.description: Using Proximal Policy Optimization to fine-tune language models against a learned reward model, with KL-divergence constraints to prevent reward hacking.

sub_topics.name: Human Preference Collection
sub_topics.description: Methods for efficiently collecting, curating, and scaling human preference annotations for training reward models and alignment objectives.

categories.name: Direct Preference Optimization
categories.description: Methods that optimize language models directly from preference data without training a separate reward model, including DPO and its variants that reparameterize the RLHF objective into a supervised learning loss.

sub_topics.name: DPO Variants
sub_topics.description: Extensions and modifications of the original DPO algorithm, including IPO, KTO, SLiC-HF, and other preference-based losses that improve upon the standard DPO formulation.

sub_topics.name: Reference-Free Methods
sub_topics.description: Preference optimization approaches that eliminate the need for a reference policy model, simplifying training and reducing memory requirements.

sub_topics.name: Online DPO
sub_topics.description: Extensions of DPO that use online-generated data rather than static offline datasets, enabling iterative improvement through on-policy sampling.

categories.name: Reward Model Design
categories.description: Architectural and methodological innovations in reward model design, including process-level supervision, outcome-based rewards, implicit reward extraction, and AI-generated constitutions for scalable oversight.

sub_topics.name: Outcome Reward Models
sub_topics.description: Reward models that evaluate the quality of final outputs or answers, providing sparse but definitive feedback signals for policy training.

sub_topics.name: Process Reward Models
sub_topics.description: Reward models that provide step-level feedback on intermediate reasoning or generation steps, enabling fine-grained credit assignment.

sub_topics.name: Implicit Rewards
sub_topics.description: Methods that derive reward signals implicitly from model behavior, log-probabilities, or self-evaluation without training an explicit reward model.

sub_topics.name: Constitutional AI
sub_topics.description: Using AI-generated principles and constitutions to provide scalable oversight and reward signals, reducing dependence on human annotators.

categories.name: RL Algorithms for LLMs
categories.description: Reinforcement learning algorithms specifically designed or adapted for fine-tuning large language models, including policy gradient methods, group-relative optimization, and actor-critic architectures scaled to LLM training.

sub_topics.name: PPO for LLMs
sub_topics.description: Adaptations and improvements of Proximal Policy Optimization for stable and efficient language model fine-tuning at scale.

sub_topics.name: GRPO
sub_topics.description: Group Relative Policy Optimization, which normalizes rewards within groups of sampled outputs to reduce variance and eliminate the need for a critic model.

sub_topics.name: REINFORCE Variants
sub_topics.description: Adaptations of the REINFORCE algorithm and its variants for language model training, including baseline subtraction and variance reduction techniques.

sub_topics.name: Actor-Critic Methods
sub_topics.description: Actor-critic architectures adapted for LLM alignment, where a separate value network provides advantage estimates for policy updates.

categories.name: Alignment Techniques
categories.description: Techniques for aligning LLM behavior with human values, safety requirements, and instruction-following capabilities, including safety training, red teaming, and multi-objective alignment approaches.

sub_topics.name: Safety Alignment
sub_topics.description: Methods for training models to refuse harmful requests, avoid generating toxic or dangerous content, and maintain safe behavior under adversarial conditions.

sub_topics.name: Instruction Following
sub_topics.description: Training models to accurately follow user instructions, including complex multi-step instructions and constraint satisfaction.

sub_topics.name: Value Alignment
sub_topics.description: Approaches for ensuring LLMs internalize and act according to human values, ethical principles, and societal norms.

sub_topics.name: Red Teaming
sub_topics.description: Adversarial testing and automated attack methods to discover and mitigate alignment failures, jailbreaks, and unsafe behaviors.

categories.name: Self-Play and Iterative Training
categories.description: Training paradigms where models generate their own training data, compete against themselves, or iteratively refine their alignment through multiple rounds of feedback and optimization.

sub_topics.name: Self-Play Fine-Tuning
sub_topics.description: Methods where LLMs improve by generating and evaluating their own responses, using self-play dynamics inspired by game-theoretic frameworks.

sub_topics.name: Iterative RLHF
sub_topics.description: Multi-round RLHF approaches where reward models and policies are alternately updated, enabling progressive improvement in alignment quality.

sub_topics.name: Online Learning and Exploration
sub_topics.description: Online RL methods that balance exploration and exploitation during LLM training, continuously improving policies with fresh on-policy data.

theme.name: Safety and Harmlessness
theme.description: Papers focused on ensuring model outputs are safe, non-toxic, and avoid harmful content generation across alignment methods.

theme.name: Helpfulness Optimization
theme.description: Papers addressing how to maximize the helpfulness and usefulness of model responses through alignment training.

theme.name: Multi-objective Alignment
theme.description: Papers dealing with balancing multiple alignment objectives such as helpfulness, harmlessness, and honesty simultaneously.

theme.name: Analysis
theme.description: Papers where experiments are conducted to evaluate the performance of baseline solutions, showing gaps and future directions.

theme.name: Benchmark
theme.description: Papers that introduce new benchmark datasets and evaluation metrics for alignment and RLHF.

theme.name: Application
theme.description: Papers that show how to apply RL-based alignment techniques to a specific domain or task, highlighting its strengths and gaps.

theme.name: Survey
theme.description: Papers that provide a comprehensive overview of the current state of the art in RL for LLMs, including its taxonomy, advantages and limitations.
